title: zinnia anomala
intro: Zinnia anomala, also known as the prairie zinnia or zinnia anomala, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Great Plains of North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as colds and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Zinnia anomala are attributed to its antiseptic, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. Herbal preparations of Zinnia anomala often involve infusions of the flowers, leaves, and roots, which can be used as teas, tinctures, or poultices to harness its medicinal properties.
